briefing.headline: Rig up a Flipping Jig — aggressive post-spawn approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for post-spawn with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on point, creek channel, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Don't soak a jig. The more flips you can get in, the more fish you get in front of, the more bites you will get. If they aren't going to eat on the first couple of hops, they can starve.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on point, creek channel, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Hudson: 'Prefer Dinger in post-spawn pitching at finicky recovering bass hanging near the surface'

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

KVD: For pitching into grass holes — 3/8oz tungsten weight with a 3/0 Mustad grip pin hook (the pin keeps the bait from sliding down). 5-inch Ocho worm. Blue craw or green pumpkin. Making it fall straight is critical — the Texas rig glides down cl...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal post-spawn bass are transitioning and aggressive. Males guard fry in 2-6ft while females roam 5-12ft feeding heavily. Target both shallow fry-guarders and roaming post-spawn females near points.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
